items.q: Boiling and simmering are exactly the same heat level.
In basic kitchen terms, what is 'cooking food briefly in boiling water then cooling'?
items.explanation: cooking food briefly in boiling water then cooling corresponds to blanching.

items.q: In basic kitchen terms, what is 'to cut ingredients into very small cubes'?
items.explanation: to cut ingredients into very small cubes corresponds to brunoise.

items.q: In basic kitchen terms, what is 'resting cooked meat does what'?
items.explanation: resting cooked meat does what corresponds to redistributes juices.

items.q: In basic kitchen terms, what is 'base mixture of chopped onion, carrot, and celery'?
items.explanation: base mixture of chopped onion, carrot, and celery corresponds to mirepoix.

items.q: Chicken is considered safely cooked at an internal temperature of 75C.
In basic kitchen terms, what is 'French term for place and prep before service'?
items.explanation: French term for place and prep before service corresponds to mise en place.

items.q: In kitchen basics, what is 'to cook in liquid just below boiling'?
In basic kitchen terms, what is 'to cook in liquid just below boiling'?
items.explanation: to cook in liquid just below boiling corresponds to poach.

items.q: In basic kitchen terms, what is 'the browning reaction on meat and bread'?
items.explanation: the browning reaction on meat and bread corresponds to Maillard reaction.
